bodyHtml:
Assessment and Evaluation CTET Notes 2026 — आकलन एवं मूल्यांकन

Assessment and Evaluation CTET Notes 2026 is one of the highest-weightage topics in CDP — carrying 26–40% of marks across CTET, KVS, DSSSB, and UPTET. यह topic सीधे तौर पर CCE, Formative/Summative Assessment, और NEP 2020 के competency-based evaluation से जुड़ा है।

Assessment refers to the process of gathering information about a student's learning progress, while Evaluation is the process of making judgments based on that information. आकलन (Assessment) एक continuous process है, जो learning को improve करने के लिए किया जाता है, जबकि मूल्यांकन (Evaluation) learning outcomes को measure करने के लिए किया जाता है।

⏱️ Estimated Read Time: 22-25 minutes • 📊 100% PYQ Coverage

📑 Table of Contents

  1. यह Topic कितना Important है? — Exam Weightage
  2. Core Concepts — आकलन और मूल्यांकन
  3. Pedagogy & NCF/NEP Angle — Exam में क्या पूछा जाता है?
  4. Current Updates — NEP 2020 & New Assessment Framework
  5. Deep Theory — Types of Assessment & Models
  6. Comparison Tables — Confusing Pairs & Classifications
  7. Mnemonics & Memory Architecture
  8. Conclusion & Next-Topic Roadmap
  9. Frequently Asked Questions — Assessment and Evaluation CTET Notes 2026

📘 यह Topic कितना Important है? — Exam Weightage

TARGET_YEAR की exam के लिए यह topic CDP का दूसरा सबसे बड़ा pillar है। अगर आपको इस topic पर command है, तो आप CDP के 30% से ज्यादा questions को एकदम confidence से solve कर सकते हैं। इस guide को पढ़ने के बाद आपको पता होगा: → CCE (Continuous and Comprehensive Evaluation) का पूरा concept clear होगा, → Formative और Summative Assessment के बीच का difference अच्छे से समझ आएगा, → NEP 2020 के अनुसार competency-based evaluation से related हर MCQ solve करने की trick मिल जाएगी।

📊 Exam Relevance Map — पिछले 5 सालों में कितने questions आए?
Exam20212022202320242025
CTET Paper I34545
CTET Paper II45456
KVS PRT34435
DSSSB TGT23544
*यह डेटा पिछले exams के analysis पर आधारित है और trend दिखाने के लिए है।

🧠 Core Concepts — आकलन और मूल्यांकन

📘 Definition — Assessment and Evaluation CTET Notes 2026
Assessment is the systematic process of collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data about a student's learning to guide instruction and improve outcomes. Evaluation is the process of making judgments about the value, effectiveness, or quality of a student's performance based on the assessment data, often for grading or certification purposes.
💡 आसान भाषा में समझें
मान लीजिए आप एक teacher हैं और आप class में पढ़ा रहे हैं। आप periodic रूप से students को questions पूछते हैं, classwork/homework कराते हैं, और उनकी progress को track करते हैं — यह आकलन (Assessment) है। जब आप इन records को देखकर कहते हैं कि "राम को math में 75% marks मिले, इसलिए वह अच्छा student है" — तो यह मूल्यांकन (Evaluation) है। Assessment प्रक्रिया (process) है, Evaluation उसका परिणाम (result) है।

1. Foundation — आकलन के मूल सिद्धांत (CTET Paper I / PRT Level)

🎯 Exam Point
  • Assessment का मुख्य उद्देश्य — student की learning में सुधार करना है (not just grading).
  • CCE (Continuous and Comprehensive Evaluation) — यह एक systematic way है, जिसमें student के academic और co-scholastic (जैसे sports, art) areas दोनों का evaluation किया जाता है.
  • Formative Assessment — classroom में daily activities, quizzes, assignments, feedback के through learning को monitor करना।
  • Summative Assessment — term-end या session-end exams, जो overall learning का certificate/grading के लिए measure करते हैं।
  • NCERT Reference: Class 3 EVS, Chapter 1 (Poonam's Day Out) — यहाँ observation और descriptive assessment का एक simple example मिलता है।

CTET Paper I में अक्सर पूछा जाता है — "एक teacher अपनी कक्षा में assessment क्यों करता है?" इसका सही answer है — "Learning को improve करने के लिए" (न कि केवल marks देने के लिए)। यह NCF 2005 का एक core principle है — "Assessment should be continuous and comprehensive, and should be used to help children learn better."

2. Intermediate — Types of Assessment & Tools (TGT / CTET Paper II Level)

💡 Did You Know?
NEP 2020 में "Competency-Based Assessment" पर जोर दिया गया है — यानी अब exams में rote learning नहीं, बल्कि student की practical skills और application-based knowledge को measure किया जाएगा। यह CTET 2026 के पेपर में भी reflect होगा।
📊 Comparison Table — Formative vs Summative Assessment
FeatureFormative AssessmentSummative Assessment
PurposeLearning को improve & guide करनाLearning outcomes को measure & certify करना
TimingDuring the learning process (daily/periodic)At the end of a unit/term/session
ToolsObservation, quizzes, assignments, projects, feedbackWritten exams, term papers, portfolios, final projects
ResultDetailed feedback (qualitative)Grades/percentage (quantitative)
🔥 PYQ Alert
CTET Dec 2022 (Paper I, CDP) — "Which of the following statements best describes 'Formative Assessment'?"
Options: (a) It is used to grade students at the end of the year. (b) It is used to give feedback to students during the learning process. (c) It is done only in mathematics. (d) It is not related to teaching-learning process.
✅ Correct Answer: (b) — It is used to give feedback to students during the learning process.
💡 Reason: Formative Assessment का purpose हर day सीखने में मदद करना है (diagnostic + remedial). इसलिए option (b) सबसे सटीक है।
📊 पूरा 38वीं–72वीं Year-wise Analysis देखें

🧠 Memory Trick available: "CCE and Types of Assessment" — Mnemonics section में देखें

⚠️ अक्सर होने वाली गलती
❌ Wrong: Formative और Summative में कोई difference नहीं है।
✅ Correct: Formative learning के दौरान होता है, Summative end पर होता है।

TGT और CTET Paper II में, assessment के tools पर ज्यादा focus होता है। उदाहरण के लिए, Observation, Portfolio, Anecdotal Records, Rubrics, Checklist, Rating Scale — ये सब Formative Assessment के important tools हैं। इनमें से हर tool का अपना specific उपयोग है, और exam में अक्सर इनके characteristics पर MCQ आते हैं।

3. Advanced — Theoretical Frameworks & NEP 2020 (PGT / KVS PGT Level)

🎓 PHD Teacher Insight
NEP 2020 ने assessment के पूरे paradigm को shift कर दिया है। पहले norm-referenced evaluation (students की तुलना एक दूसरे से) पर focus था, अब criterion-referenced evaluation (students की तुलना उनके own learning objectives से) पर focus है। इसका मतलब है — अब exams में "अगले से बेहतर कौन" नहीं बल्कि "क्या student ने अपने level पर mastery हासिल की" यह पूछा जाएगा। यह Vygotsky के ZPD (Zone of Proximal Development) theory से भी connect है, जहाँ assessment को "scaffolding" के रूप में देखा जाता है।
📊 Advanced Comparison — Norm-Referenced vs Criterion-Referenced Evaluation
FeatureNorm-ReferencedCriterion-Referenced
PurposeStudents को एक दूसरे से compare करनाStudents की own learning objectives के against performance measure करना
FocusRelative position (rank)Mastery of content
ExamplePercentile rank, standardized tests (like SAT)Pass/Fail based on a cut-off score, learning competency exams
🎯 Exam Point
  • NEP 2020 Clause 4.34 — "The progress card of a student must be a holistic, multidimensional report that reflects the student's progress in cognitive, affective, and psychomotor domains."
  • NCF 2023 — "Assessment should be used to provide constructive feedback and should be an integral part of the teaching-learning process, not an external add-on."
  • Competency-based learning — इस approach में assessment का focus "learning outcomes" पर होता है, न कि "content coverage" पर।
💡 Did You Know?
"Assessment for Learning" (Formative), "Assessment of Learning" (Summative), और "Assessment as Learning" (students खुद अपनी learning को monitor करें) — ये तीनों approaches NEP 2020 के assessment framework का हिस्सा हैं। CTET 2026 में "Assessment as Learning" से related questions भी आ सकते हैं!

🧠 Memory Trick available: "Norm-Referenced vs Criterion-Referenced" — Mnemonics section में देखें

🎓 Pedagogy & NCF/NEP Angle — Exam में क्या पूछा जाता है?

3A. Exam में यह पूछा जाता है — एक Teacher को क्या करना चाहिए?
  • Constructivist approach में teacher की भूमिका: teacher को facilitator बनना चाहिए, जो assessment को learning का हिस्सा बनाए (Piaget और Vygotsky के अनुसार).
  • Activity-based learning: teacher को ऐसी activities design करनी चाहिए जो students को explore करने दें (जैसे group projects, experiments).
  • Bloom's Taxonomy in Assessment: MCQ में अक्सर पूछा जाता है कि किस level (Remember, Understand, Apply, Analyze, Evaluate, Create) का assessment किस tool से होगा।
  • Common Student Error: students अक्सर "revision के लिए assessment" को "grading के लिए assessment" confuse कर देते हैं। teacher को इसे clear करना चाहिए और feedback देने पर focus करना चाहिए।
  • Inclusive Education: teacher को सभी students (CWSN, diverse backgrounds) के लिए appropriate assessment tools का use करना चाहिए, जैसे oral tests, visual aids के साथ assignments.
📰 Updates Corner (Last 12–18 Months)
As reported by Ministry of Education, September 2025: NEP 2020 के तहत, सभी states में "Competency-Based Assessment" framework लागू कर दिया गया है। इसमें students की practical skills और real-world application को measure किया जाएगा।
→ Exam Impact: CTET 2026 में इस framework से related questions (जैसे "Which assessment tool is best for measuring competency?") आ सकते हैं।
As reported by NCERT, June 2025: NCF 2023 के अनुसार, Class 3-5 में "Portfolio Assessment" को mandatory कर दिया गया है, ताकि students की holistic progress को track किया जा सके।
→ Exam Impact: इससे "Portfolio Assessment" पर आने वाले MCQs की संख्या बढ़ सकती है।
📦 Section Summary
✅ Assessment learning को improve करता है, Evaluation outcomes को measure करता है
✅ Formative (during) vs Summative (end) — purpose और timing में difference
✅ NEP 2020 का focus Competency-Based & Criterion-Referenced Evaluation पर है
✅ Teacher को facilitator की भूमिका में assessment को holistic और inclusive बनाना चाहिए

🔬 Deep Theory — Types of Assessment & Models

Assessment के कई models हैं, जिन्हें exams में अक्सर compare किया जाता है। यहाँ हम 4 major types को detail में समझेंगे:

Types of Assessment Flowchart — CTET 2026 के लिए

Diagnostic Assessment (Before Teaching) ↓
Formative Assessment (During Teaching) ↓
Summative Assessment (End of Unit) ↓
Evaluative Judgment (Grading/Feedback)
📊 Ability/Description Table — Assessment Tools
Assessment Tool Description — किस काम आता है?
🔑 ObservationTeacher student की behavior और skills को daily activities में देखता है — इससे non-academic skills (जैसे teamwork) भी assess होती हैं।
🔑 PortfolioStudent के best work का collection (drawings, assignments, projects) — जो progress को time के साथ दिखाता है।
🔑 ChecklistA list of specific criteria (जैसे "student ने questions correctly solved?") को mark करना — यह quick और objective है।
🔑 RubricDetailed scoring guide जो performance को different levels (Excellent, Good, Satisfactory) में बाँटता है — यह complex tasks (जैसे essay) के लिए best है।
🔑 Anecdotal RecordTeacher का written note about a significant incident or behavior — यह qualitative data provide करता है।
💡 Did You Know?
"Authentic Assessment" एक concept है जो real-world tasks पर focus करता है (जैसे project-based learning में)। यह NEP 2020 के competency-based approach से मेल खाता है। CTET 2026 में "Authentic Assessment" के examples पर MCQ आ सकते हैं!
🔥 PYQ Alert
KVS PRT 2023 (CDP) — "What is the primary purpose of a portfolio assessment?"
Options: (a) To measure student's performance at the end of the year. (b) To showcase a student's best work and progress over time. (c) To compare students with each other. (d) To give marks only.
✅ Correct Answer: (b) — To showcase a student's best work and progress over time.
💡 Reason: Portfolio का focus holistic progress और self-reflection पर होता है, न कि केवल final marks पर।
📊 पूरा 38वीं–72वीं Year-wise Analysis देखें

🧠 Memory Trick available: "Portfolio vs Rubric vs Checklist" — Mnemonics section में देखें

📊 Comparison Tables — Confusing Pairs & Classifications

📊 Assessment vs Evaluation — क्या difference है?
ParameterAssessmentEvaluation
ProcessGathering dataMaking judgments based on data
GoalImprove learningMeasure and certify outcomes
ResultFeedback (qualitative)Grade/Score (quantitative)
Classification Tree — Types of Evaluation Evaluation ├── Formative (Assessment for Learning) │ ├── Tools: Observation, Quizzes, Assignments │ └── Focus: Feedback & Improvement ├── Summative (Assessment of Learning) │ ├── Tools: Term Exams, Portfolios, Final Projects │ └── Focus: Certification & Grading └── Diagnostic (Assessment BEFORE Learning) ├── Tools: Pre-tests, Baseline surveys └── Focus: Identify prior knowledge & learning gaps

🧠 Mnemonics & Memory Architecture — इस Topic के सभी Mnemonics

🧠 इस Topic के सभी Mnemonics
🔒 CCE and Types of Assessment FADSE... Complete...
🔒 Norm-Referenced vs Criterion-Referenced NCC... Rule...
🔒 Portfolio vs Rubric vs Checklist PRC... Quick...
🎬 Visual Association Story — A Day in Classroom Assessment एक दिन teacher ने...
🔓 इस Topic के सभी 4 Mnemonics + Visual Story सिर्फ Memory Maps में उपलब्ध हैं → Memory Maps देखें

📘 Conclusion & Next-Topic Roadmap

📦 Section Summary
✅ Assessment और Evaluation में अंतर clear करें — Assessment process है, Evaluation judgment है
✅ Formative (learning के दौरान) और Summative (end पर) — दोनों के different purposes हैं
✅ NEP 2020 और NCF 2023 के competency-based framework से related questions TARGET_YEAR में जरूर आएंगे
✅ Tools (Observation, Portfolio, Checklist, Rubric) — इनके definitions और uses याद रखें
✅ Criterion-Referenced पर focus बढ़ा है — यह norm-referenced से बेहतर है (NEP 2020 view)
⚡ 20 Second Revision — Assessment and Evaluation CTET Notes 2026
• Assessment = Data Gathering → Formative (during) • Summative (end)
• Evaluation = Judgment → Norm-Referenced (compare) vs Criterion-Referenced (mastery)
• Tools: Observation, Portfolio, Checklist, Rubric, Anecdotal Record
• NEP 2020 → Competency-Based & Holistic Progress Report
• Teacher's Role → Facilitator, inclusive, feedback-oriented
📘 Related Topics Roadmap
Now that you've mastered Assessment and Evaluation CTET Notes 2026, your next priority should be:
  • 1. Learning Process and Principles CTET Notes 2026 — यह topic सीधे assessment से connect है, क्योंकि assessment का purpose learning को improve करना है। यहाँ आप learning theories (Piaget, Vygotsky) को समझेंगे। Study Time: 2.5 Hours
  • 2. Intelligence and Personality CTET Notes 2026 — इस topic में आप individual differences को समझेंगे, जो assessment को tailor करने में मदद करता है (जैसे CWSN के लिए special assessment tools)। Study Time: 2 Hours
  • 3. Inclusive Education CTET Notes 2026 — यहाँ आप CWSN (Children with Special Needs) और inclusive assessment practices के बारे में पढ़ेंगे — यह NEP 2020 का एक key area है। Study Time: 2 Hours

Assessment and Evaluation को और गहराई से समझने के लिए NCERT's "Teachers' Package on Continuous and Comprehensive Evaluation" और NEP 2020 (Section 4 on Assessment) के official resources देखें।

Recommended Study Path: Learning Process → Intelligence & Personality → Inclusive Education.

❓ Frequently Asked Questions — Assessment and Evaluation CTET Notes 2026

What is the difference between Assessment and Evaluation in CTET CDP?

Assessment is the process of collecting data to improve learning (formative, ongoing), while Evaluation is the process of making judgments based on that data to grade or certify (summative, outcome-focused).

What is CCE in Assessment and Evaluation CTET Notes 2026?

CCE (Continuous and Comprehensive Evaluation) is a system that assesses both academic and co-scholastic areas continuously, providing holistic feedback to improve student learning.

Which assessment tools are most commonly asked in CTET 2026?

Observation, Portfolio, Checklist, Rubric, and Anecdotal Records are the most frequently tested tools, especially their purposes and uses in formative assessment.

How does NEP 2020 impact Assessment and Evaluation CTET Notes 2026?

NEP 2020 shifts focus to competency-based, criterion-referenced evaluation, emphasizing holistic, multidimensional progress reports over norm-referenced comparisons.

What is the syllabus for Assessment and Evaluation in CTET Paper I 2026?

It includes concepts like Formative/Summative Assessment, CCE, tools (Observation, Portfolio, etc.), NEP 2020/NCF 2023 frameworks, and teacher's role in inclusive assessment.

**Topic:** CCE and Types of Assessment
**Mnemonic:** "FADE" — Formative, Assessment for Learning, Diagnostic, Evaluation (Summative)
**How to Recall:** FADE helps you remember the four key phases: Formative (during), Assessment (gathering data), Diagnostic (before), Evaluation (end judgment).
**Exam Speed:** 3s
**Used In:** Memory Trick pointer in Section 2 (Core Concepts)

**Topic:** Norm-Referenced vs Criterion-Referenced
**Mnemonic:** "NCC" — Norm vs Criterion: Compare vs Criteria
**How to Recall:** NCC stands for Norm-Referenced (Compare with peers) vs Criterion-Referenced (Compare against fixed criteria/mastery).
**Exam Speed:** 2s
**Used In:** Memory Trick pointer in Section 2 (Core Concepts)

**Topic:** Portfolio vs Rubric vs Checklist
**Mnemonic:** "PRC" — Portfolio (Process + Reflection), Rubric (Criteria + Levels), Checklist (Quick + Objective)
**How to Recall:** PRC helps you recall the three tools: Portfolio is a collection over time (Process), Rubric has detailed levels (Criteria), Checklist is a simple binary list (Quick).
**Exam Speed:** 3s
**Used In:** Memory Trick pointer in Section 5 (Deep Theory)

**Topic:** Visual Association Story — A Day in Classroom Assessment
**Mnemonic:** Visual story of a teacher named "Ms. CCE" who walks into class and uses four tools: (1) She **observes** students in a group activity (Observation), (2) Then she reviews their **portfolios** to track progress (Portfolio), (3) She uses a **checklist** to mark completed tasks (Checklist), and (4) Finally, she gives a **rubric** for a project to score them (Rubric). The story ends with her providing **feedback** (Formative) and later assigning a **grade** (Summative).
**How to Recall:** Picture "Ms. CCE" doing the actions in order: Observe → Portfolio → Checklist → Rubric. Each tool is a mental image: seeing students (Observation), flipping through a folder (Portfolio), ticking boxes on a clipboard (Checklist), and looking at a multi-level scoring sheet (Rubric).
**Exam Speed:** 4s
**Used In:** Section 7 (Mnemonics & Memory Architecture)
